name: Estrogen / endocrine-disruptor sensor (ERα + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects estrogens and estrogenic endocrine-disrupting chemicals in water. Uses the human estrogen receptor (ERα) expressed in GRAS baker's yeast — a eukaryotic sensing class bacteria cannot do — gating a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter.

input.detects: 17β-estradiol and estrogen-mimicking endocrine disruptors in water
input.operatingRange: Yeast ERα assays detect sub-nM to nM 17β-estradiol

sensing.sensorMechanism: Ligand-bound human ERα binds estrogen-response elements (EREs), activating an ERE-controlled promoter (the classic yeast estrogen screen).
sensing.signalFlow: Estrogen -> ERα binds ERE promoter -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-VPR amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises with estrogenic activity.

parts.name: Human estrogen receptor alpha (ERα)
parts.source: Human ESR1, expressed in yeast (Routledge & Sumpter 1996, yeast estrogen screen)
parts.notes: Ligand-activated nuclear receptor — requires a eukaryotic host.

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak yeast promoter

performance.notes: The yeast estrogen screen (ERα + ERE) is a validated assay; the CRISPRa-amplified version here is a design template. Reports total estrogenic activity (a key advantage for unknown EDC mixtures).

safety.notes: GRAS baker's yeast; eukaryotic chassis enables human-receptor-based sensing.

buildSteps.title: Design CRISPRa sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target a weak yeast promoter; check S. cerevisiae off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: Express ERα; ERE -> sgRNA; dCas9-VPR; weak promoter -> yEGFP. Use the Yeast Toolkit (MoClo).

buildSteps.title: Transform S. cerevisiae
buildSteps.description: Integrate at defined loci; select on auxotrophic markers.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the water sample + estradiol standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the yeast estrogen screen (ERα/ERE) with eukaryotic CRISPRa
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
